hero.title: Agent supply + AI-native organization incubation
hero.description: Build Agents as supplyable capability units, validate them against real work, publish them to a governed market, and incubate AI-native organizations around AI partners.
hero.proof: Build · Verify · Release · Install · Run · Evolve

console.title: Agent supply invoked by the business
console.goal: Resolve and document a multilingual customer escalation
console.formula: Customer knowledge · Multilingual content · Data analysis · System action · Policy
console.checkpoint: Review external responses, privileged actions, and policy boundaries.
console.deliverable: Completed actions, outcome summary, approval record, and inspectable evidence

solutions.title: Supply Agents. Incubate AI-native organizations.
solutions.description: Start with enterprise Agent supply, form an OPC around AI partners, or design a higher-education digital employee pilot from composable platform capabilities.

items.title: Enterprise Agent supply
items.description: Turn business knowledge, methods, tools, and policies into standardized Agents that teams can verify, release, install, operate, and improve.
items.chain: Build → Verify → Release → Install → Run → Evolve
items.outcome: Versioned Agents, verification evidence, installation policies, operating records, and evolution plans.

items.title: OPC incubation
items.description: Incubate an OPC where a human core works with AI partners formed by one Agent or a coordinated combination of Agents.
items.chain: Define the business → Shape AI partners → Establish collaboration → Automate recurring work → Govern execution → Scale
items.outcome: A human-led team with clear AI partner roles, operating rhythms, governance boundaries, and measurable delivery.

items.title: Higher-education digital employees
items.description: Design role-based pilots for teaching, research, student services, and administration by combining composable platform capabilities with institutional knowledge and governance.
items.chain: Identify roles → Configure knowledge → Validate tasks → Pilot with one team → Govern service → Improve continuously
items.outcome: A validated pilot blueprint aligned to campus roles, institutional knowledge, approval boundaries, and service evidence.

capabilities.title: The operating system for Agent supply and AI-native teams
capabilities.description: Five connected capabilities move Agents from creation into governed organizational use while keeping people, AI partners, automation, and evidence in one operating environment.

items.title: Agent factory
items.description: Build, verify, version, and release Agents from reusable methods, knowledge, tools, models, and policies.

items.title: Agent market
items.description: Discover approved Agents, inspect their capabilities and permissions, and install the right version for each team.

items.title: Collaboration workspace
items.description: Give people and AI partners shared goals, context, conversations, artifacts, decisions, and delivery evidence.

items.title: Automation
items.description: Trigger recurring work, coordinate Agent combinations, track progress, and route decisions to the right people.

items.title: Governance
items.description: Control permissions, execution boundaries, approvals, budgets, versions, evidence, and audit history.

operating.title: A closed loop from build to evolution
operating.description: Each Agent is a supplyable capability unit. The platform carries it through a governed lifecycle, then uses operating evidence to improve the next version and the organization around it.

parts.title: Build
parts.description: Assemble a focused Agent from business methods, knowledge, tools, models, and policies.

parts.title: Verify
parts.description: Test real tasks, failure paths, permissions, and acceptance evidence before release.

parts.title: Release
parts.description: Release an approved, versioned Agent to the governed Agent market.

parts.title: Install
parts.description: Configure the approved version for a team, role, environment, and access scope.

parts.title: Run
parts.description: Operate the Agent alone or as part of an AI partner with visible progress and human checkpoints.

parts.title: Evolve
parts.description: Use outcomes, feedback, incidents, and new knowledge to validate the next version.

operating.humanTitle: AI partners turn Agent supply into organization
operating.humanDescription: An AI partner can be one Agent or a coordinated Agent combination. An OPC keeps humans at the core while AI partners expand the team's delivery capacity.

humanItems.title: Agent
humanItems.description: The smallest capability unit that can be built, validated, published, installed, run, and evolved.

humanItems.title: AI partner
humanItems.description: A business-facing Agent or Agent combination with a clear role, context, and delivery responsibility.

humanItems.title: OPC
humanItems.description: A human core and AI partners operating as one accountable, AI-native team.

market.title: Release once. Install governed Agent supply where it is needed.
market.description: The Agent market distributes reusable capability; it is not a solution domain. Teams can inspect scope, evidence, permissions, and versions before installation.

apps.title: Software delivery Agent
apps.description: Carry requirements through implementation, verification, review, and accepted code delivery.

apps.title: Multi-Agent orchestration
apps.description: Coordinate specialized Agents around one goal, shared checkpoints, and one accepted result.

apps.title: Dual-repository sync Agent
apps.description: Detect divergence, synchronize GitLab and GitHub, and route changes through the correct review path.

market.plannedDescription: New Agents enter the market only after their role, permissions, failure paths, deliverables, and operating evidence are verified.

trust.title: Run Agent supply inside enterprise boundaries
trust.description: Every Agent run stays constrained by infrastructure, isolated workspaces, credential scope, approval policy, budgets, and evidence requirements.

items.title: Runner deployment control
items.description: Deploy Runners inside infrastructure and network boundaries defined by the organization.

items.title: Isolated workspaces
items.description: Separate each assignment, its files, dependencies, and execution context.

items.title: Scoped credentials
items.description: Grant each Agent only the access required for its approved role and task.

items.title: Inspectable evidence
items.description: Retain activity, decisions, approvals, artifacts, versions, and delivery status.

trust.runtimesDescription: Runners host approved Agent runtimes including Codex CLI, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Aider, and OpenCode. Availability follows the verified runtime catalog.

cta.title: Build your first supplyable Agent
cta.description: Choose one real business role, verify its work, release the approved version, and expand from one Agent into an AI partner or OPC.
